title: Review Diversification and Specificity: Attributes
content_md:
# Review Diversification and Specificity: Attributes

- Code: 21dcf3ad
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=21dcf3ad0683465e8890496f6e8152b8
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/2a510e201f734ca985414a34469f6c92?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 3m 12s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So there are two aspects that I really wanted to hit on here for reviews as it pertains to AI systems
You guys probably seen recently Google announced this ask maps feature
But this was an issue an opportunity even before this came out
Basically, we want our clients or our businesses to show up
recommended by AI systems and ask maps is essentially Google's AI system inside maps recommending local businesses
This happened anyway in the AI overview, but certainly we're going more and more toward AI recommendations and
Less toward the map pack. Plus, of course, we have chat GPT cloud perplexity, etc. Etc
Recommending local businesses. So this is for AI systems Google business profile reviews are not the most important
They're not the most important for Google's AI. They're not the most important for chat GPT or any others
Okay, obviously if we're talking about the map pack all we care about is Google
Business profile reviews, but we move beyond that. So do not ask just for GBP reviews
Okay, what we're doing for our clients is we're getting them to request reviews across a diverse array of platforms
Still the GBP, but Yelp is a close close number two
And in fact, if your main focus is optimizing for AI systems Yelp should be your number one
Google's AI overview prefers Yelp reviews to Google reviews as do others
Okay, so in addition to Yelp, there's also Facebook reviews
There's Bing reviews. There's and then there's a variety of other local platforms including just being active on Reddit
Which will cover in a different lesson. So that's the main one of the two main aspects here
Is that you want to make sure you're diversifying your reviews beyond that?
Beyond diversifying your reviews when Google added this ask maps and started to focus more and more on AI systems
We know that Google's AI is starting to work a lot like chat GPT's this isn't a surprise
So chat GPT's AI will read all the reviews that it can it often will miss GBP reviews
But it will read all the reviews that it can and look to specifically match
What someone is asking for against what is in the reviews?
For example, if someone goes to chat GPT and says hey recommend the coffee shop with fast Wi-Fi
Then chat GPT will actually go and read reviews looking to see if any of the reviews mentioned fast Wi-Fi
Ask maps will do the same thing with GBP reviews
So when you're asking for reviews or ask for reviews, you can't just ask for a five-star review anymore
You want the person leaving the review to be specific
We used to just give the clients a text message that was just like hey, would you mind leaving us a quick review?
Now we're asking hey, thanks for choosing us. Would you mind sharing what we fixed and how it went?
We're trying to encourage the person leaving the review to be much more specific on what happened and what was fixed
So that's it review specificity and a diversification of review platforms important for AI systems
